text: You have three hours (plus any pre-agreed individual adjustment) to complete the paper.
Answer four essay questions, TWO from Section A and TWO from Section B.
Each essay question carries the same number of marks.
Candidates are advised to devote equal time to each question.

This is an open book exam.
Candidates should not copy and paste images from any other documents.
Strictly observe the word limit of 1125 words per essay.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer TWO questions from SECTION A and TWO questions from SECTION B.
content: Discuss the roles played by innate lymphoid cells (ILC1, ILC2, ILC3 and NK cells) in controlling infection.
Discuss how thymic T cell development balances the need to recognise diverse pathogens while minimising potential for autoimmunity.
Using examples from both chronic and acute infections, describe how viruses evade detection by their human and animal hosts.
Discuss the factors affecting the transmission of viruses between hosts.
Using specific diseases as examples, discuss how evasion of host defences by pathogenic bacteria can lead to chronic infection.
Compare and contrast the historical and regional differences in treatment, control and elimination of schistosomiasis and malaria.
How does inflammation contribute to atherogenesis and the spectrum of atheromatous disease?
